rationale.R1.3: The text reports KL(p_router ‖ p_oracle) = 0.92 for StanRoute vs. 0.47 for the heuristic baseline, then calls this 'favorable' for StanRoute. KL divergence is a discrepancy measure: lower is better.
rationale.RX.1: I audited each empirical claim against the reported numbers and flagged several places where the evidence does not support — and in one case directly contradicts — what the text concludes.
rationale.RX.3: The argument structure is: code generation shows a 6.6% relative gain → therefore StanRoute 'uniformly improves routing across all RouterEval task families' → therefore probabilistic routers 'should replace heuristic routers in general-purpose LLM serving deployments at any operating point on the cost-quality frontier.'
rationale.RX.4: Evaluation is on one benchmark (RouterEval-2024), one calibration split, one training run (5,000 SVI steps, single guide fit), and one baseline ('the heuristic-router baseline,' unspecified). Generalization to 'any cost-conscious LLM application' is not supported.
rationale.RX.5: The oracle is defined as 'the cheapest model achieving a correct output,' but no information is given on how correctness was determined per family (especially for creative writing and dialogue, where there is no obvious ground truth).
